steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question asking what topic both texts share. Text 1 argues the word "curry" should be abandoned due to its colonial origins and inaccuracy. Text 2 describes a chef who, despite acknowledging the colonial roots of the term, continues to use it. Both texts center on the same debate about one specific word.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The difference between korma and rendang: Text 1 mentions korma and rendang only as a brief opening example; Text 2 does not mention them at all. This is a minor detail, not the shared topic. ✗
  • Choice B — The best method for studying a region's cuisine: Neither text discusses methodology for studying cuisine. This topic does not appear in either passage. ✗
  • Choice C — The history about the peoples on the Indian subcontinent: Both texts touch on the Indian subcontinent, but that is background context, not what the texts are actually debating. ✗
  • Choice D — The use of the term "curry": Text 1 argues for abandoning "curry" because of its colonial roots and imprecision; Text 2 describes a chef who acknowledges those roots but still uses the term. Both texts take positions on whether "curry" should be used — that is the shared topic. ✓
That is the correct answer.
